steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "Fans of the film ______ the commanding bass voice behind the character Dr. Facilier." The subject is 'Fans' — a plural noun. The blank must be a verb that agrees with that plural subject in a simple present-tense context.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'recognizes': third-person singular — used with 'he,' 'she,' or 'it,' not a plural subject like 'Fans.' ✗
  • Choice B — 'has recognized': singular present perfect — 'has' is a singular auxiliary and does not agree with the plural 'Fans.' ✗
  • Choice C — 'recognize': base-form plural present tense — agrees with the plural subject 'Fans.' ✓
  • Choice D — 'is recognizing': singular present progressive — 'is' is a singular auxiliary and does not agree with the plural 'Fans.' ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
